source_title: A double-slit which-way experiment on the complementarity-uncertainty debate
source_authors: Mir; Lundeen; Mitchell; Steinberg; Wiseman; Garretson
gate_question: Does the source provide a measured record distribution and a paired visibility-loss curve suitable for a no-refit distribution-to-visibility test?

figures.scout_role: closest measured distribution analogue to Xiao

figures.observable: quantum eraser conditional WVP and interference for +45 degree polarizer
figures.scout_role: eraser phase-control evidence, not a visibility sweep

figures.observable: quantum eraser conditional WVP and interference for -45 degree polarizer
figures.scout_role: eraser phase-control evidence, not a visibility sweep

source_text_findings: The paper reports direct observation of a weak-valued momentum-transfer distribution.
Figure 3 plots P_wv(q) and a variance integral, giving a real measured distribution target.
Figure 4 gives quantum-eraser conditional patterns, but not a controlled visibility-loss sweep.
The current source therefore does not clear the Xiao-like no-refit gate.
